ocr_text:
Leone is Chosen to Run For State Supreme Court By Brooklyn Democrats
Bklyn Dem Party leaders, having chosen Boro Pres Sebastian Leone to run for NYS Sup Ct in Nov, reptdly have chosen City Councilman Howard Golden to succeed Leone if he is elected (S)

Harvard Researcher Tells Medical Aides That Reducing Tension Will Help Combat Disease
Carter addresses Amer Public Health Assn luncheon on Oct 19, Miami Beach, Fla (S)

Letters to the Editor
Dorothy Davis lr comments on Oct 14 article on rept prepared by internatl economists on future of world economy

Class and Caution
T Wicker, noting forecasts that Carter will be elected Pres, views Jimmy Carter as candidate of indelible 'class sense' in that he has strong identification with poor, deprived, ill-treated, victims of social, econ or legal injustice; holds his ingrained class sense, ingrained caution and obviously sharp intelligence are most striking characteristics; suggests that if Carter apptmts should reflect his class sense, his Adm might conceivably produce most constructive change since New Deal

Race for the Presidency Narrows In New York, Jersey, Connecticut
illus of Ford campaigning in Houston, Tex, on Oct 30

Now: The Ripcord Olympics
J Durso on upcoming XXI World Parachuting Championships, Rome, Italy
Now: The Ripcord Olympics

Carter Will Press In All the States For Wide Mandate
Jimmy Carter says he will press for wide mandate rather than concentrating on narrow number of states that might guarantee him victory; wants to return to personal campaign style he used in primaries; Carter campaign staff seen reluctant to narrow campaign's focus as long as polls show him to be so strong in all regions of county; allocation of campaign funds as limited by new campaign financing law also seen as factor (M)
Nantucket's Lure Offsets Toil for 2 Innkeepers

Detroit's Public Schools Open a Lean Year
Detroit pss open to lean year, as result of budget cutbacks which forced teacher layoffs and curtailment or elimination of many services and and programs; Mich State Educ Bd orders Detroit officials to restore full day's program to 1st graders, but School Supt Arthur Jefferson notes difficulty of complying with order; interscholastic athletics program, eliminated in '75 budget cuts, is restored after appeals from professional athletes and various orgns; illus (M)

Retired Leader Of Steelworkers Misses Limelight
Int with former USWA pres David J McDonald; illus (M)
Cooking Up a Varied Batch of Classes for the Fall Season

China and America
James Reston suggests Chinese mourn him without internatl pomp because their view of man's relationship with life and with nature differs sharply from the 'raging science' of the West; comments that Mao for all his great leadership was unable to organize pol succession to assure endurance of his philosophy (M)

Brazil Moves Toward State Capitalism
Article on Brazilian moves toward new model of development 2 yrs after soaring oil prices and world recession brought 'econ miracle' to end; cites greater role for state in econ, new emphasis on Brazilian-made goods to replace imports and slower rate of econ growth during next few yrs as emerging pattern; says Pres Ernesto Geisel views approaching munic elections in Nov as test of his prestige and policies; says Geisel appears intent on carrying out modest pol liberalization; describes mil Govt's hold on power as indication that there is little likelihood that econ crisis and pol tensions will lead to unrest; illus (L)

Text of ‘Declaration’
complete text of 'Declaration of Hq of Croatian Natl Liberation Forces'
Team USA Loses, 4‐2, To Canada

Spanish Prime Minister Pledges Strong 2‐Unit Parliament by June
Spanish Prime Min Adolfo Suarez Gonzales pledges strong 2-unit Parliament that will have power to change Const, speech (M)
Arnold Palmer at 47: Should He Quit or Keep Struggling on Tour?

12 States Hold Primaries Today; Kennedy and Humphrey Opposed
12 states hold primary elections Sept 14 for Cong and state offices; most attention has been focused on NYS, where 5 Dems are seeking their party's nomination for seat of Sen James Buckley, who is opposed for renomination on Repub line; Sens Hubert Humphrey and Edward Kennedy have primary contests, but neither is expected to have difficulty in winning renomination (M)

Badillo and Velez Trade Charges Of ‘Dictator’
Badillo accuses Velez of running 'dictatorship' in S Bronx that includes 'intimidation' of poverty workers, campaign debate; Velez accuses Badillo of being 'liar' and conducting 'smear campaign'; challenges Badillo to turn over any evidence of wrongdoing to authorities (S)

City Planning Commission Approves the Construction of a Hotel incorporating Landmark Villard House
NYC City Planning Comm approves construction of 51‐story luxury hotel incorporating landmark Villard Houses behind St Patrick's Cathedral; is satisfied with program for preservation of 19th‐century town houses, agreed to by developer Harry B Helmsley; Comr Chester Rapkin dissents; hotel design, by Emery Roth, has undergone extensive revision to win acceptance by Landmarks Comm and private preservationist groups (M)

Party Balance in Senate Expected to Change Little; Forecasts Now Indicate Democratic Gain of 2 Seats
illus of Mamie Eisenhower at Eisenhower Coll, where she dedicated new athletic center (S)
Hawaiians Are Choosing Nominees For Seat in the U.S. Senate Today

ARKANSAS GOVERNOR TO CURB STATE'S AID
Gov David H Pryor to ask State Legis in Jan to hold increased spending for educ to minimum; teacher-opposition to proposed cuts expected (S)

Moynihan Urges Manufacturers That Are Planning to Leave State of New York to Postpone Moves
NYS Dem candidate Daniel P Moynihan, speaking outside Westinghouse plant in Buffalo, urges mfrs planning to leave state to delay move for 6 mos in order to give Dem adm a chance to make move unnecessary; co plans to close part of plant and move it out of state (S)

Vermont Governor in Close Democratic Senate Race As 4 New England States Hold Primaries Tuesday
public interest atty Scott Skinner seen mounting effective challenge to Gov Thomas P Salmon in bid for Vt US Sen nomination; Treas Stella Hackel apparently leads Dem field in bid to succeed Salmon as Gov, but is seen likely to spur Dem liberals to defect to Repub favorite, Richard Snelling; ex-Gov Philip Hoff calls her 'reactionary'; Lt Gov Brian Burns and Sen Robert O'Brien divide liberal vote against her (M)

Democrats Expected to Maintain Control of Congressional Delegation
pol observers, analyzing why Dems are expected to retain lopsided control of NJ Cong delegation in Nov, list as among major problems facing Repubs fact that Williams is heavily favored over Norcross (S)

Helstoski Defeats Baer in Rerun Of Jersey Congressional Primary
Helstoski, despite Fed indictment, wins renomination in special Dem primary election in NJ's 9th Cong Dist, defeating Baer by surprisingly decisive margin; with virtually all votes counted, tally is 19,483 to 15,830; Helstoski illus (L)

A Tight Race in Texas
Tom Wicker sees Carter facing tight race in Texas
Coach Hayes Defends His Late‐Punt Strategy

Forsythe and Rival Debate on Jobs
Rev of HR race in NJ's 6th Cong Dist between conservative Repub incumbent Edwin B Forsythe and Dem challenger Catherine A Costa, Burlington County Freeholder; Forsythe is favorite; their illus; map shows dist boundaries (M)
About the World Series
About the World Series

Virginia, a State With Many Changes, is Still Expected to Return Byrd to the Senate
Article on US Sen race in Va between Sen Harry F Byrd Jr and Adm Elmo Zumwalt; Byrd is heavily favored to win even though his late father's Dem pol machine has come apart in 10 yrs since his death; Martin Perper, Repub running as ind, is given no chance in race (M)

Ex‐Sports Stars Are Set for Race—Political, That Is
Distance runner Wes Santee's bid as Repub nominee for HR cited in article on number of athletes seeking pol office (S)

Dole Predicts Carter Losses in the South
Sen Dole predicts Jimmy Carter will lose Southern support as his ‘liberal’ philosophy becomes more apparent, Aug 31 airport press conf, Wilmington, Del, at beginning of campaign swing through Ga and NC; Dole illus with Repub Natl Chmn Mary Louise Smith (M)

Letters to the Editor
Andrew Fitch scores Sec Kissinger for ordering mil buildup in S Korea following killing of 2 Amer mil officers in demilitarized zone by N Korean guards (S)

Delaware's Flood Potential Held Less Than Predicted
NJ environmental officials conclude that potential destruction from future floods of Delaware River may not be as widespread as predicted; are preparing to reduce amount of low‐lying land that was designated in Jan for strict land‐use controls; illus (L)

The Medicaid Scandal
Article on increased cohabitation by single persons in US and increased tolerance of cts to situation; some suits cited (L)

People and Business
PPG Industries chmn Robinson F Barker says extension of time period over which EPA intends to reduce lead content of gasoline would prevent gasoline shortage next summer and allow refiners to make more gasoline out of each bbl of crude oil processed, lr to Admr Russell E Train; cites studies predicting 500,000‐bbl shortage of gasoline next summer, which, he says, could cause $5.2‐billion reduction in GNP, $4.1‐billion reduction in consumer spending, loss of at least 45,000 jobs, and drop in new‐car sales of over 200,000 units; PPG manufactures lead additives (M)

Concern for National Security Rising in U.S., Poll. Indicates
New survey of Amer attitudes toward foreign-policy issues, assembled by Potomac Assocs and published in Foreign Policy magazine, finds that majority feel there was decline in Adm's handling of negotiations toward Middle East settlement (S)

Brezhnev Expects Crops To Cover Domestic Need
Brezhnev says nation's meat shortage is continuing, speech to party and econ officials in Alma-Ata (M)
Brezhnev Expects Crops To Cover Domestic Need
Carter, in Apparent Shift, Stresses Curb on Inflation

LABOR DAY EXODUS GETS UNDER WAY
Natl Safety Council pres Vincent Tofany predicts there will be 360 to 460 traffic fatalities during Labor Day weekend (S)
